reasoning: With only 10 days remaining before the December 31 deadline, Russian forces are reported to occupy positions in southeastern Lyman as of December 21. While they have successfully breached the city limits, advancing through urban terrain to secure the central train station on Vulytsya Pryvokzal'na within 10 days is highly difficult. Historical baselines for Russian urban advances (e.g., Chasiv Yar, Selydove) suggest that overcoming 1-2 km of urban resistance typically takes weeks rather than days, unless Ukrainian defenses suddenly collapse. The market probability was 0.12 on December 11 (before they entered the city), so the probability is slightly higher now due to their foothold, but strictly bounded by the rapidly expiring clock. I estimate a 17% chance they capture the station in time.
